title: Doellingeria elegans
intro: Doellingeria elegans, also known as Nodding Bur Marigold, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the aster family (Asteraceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Doellingeria elegans include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, Doellingeria elegans is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in folk remedies for fever reduction and cough relief.
taxonomy: Doellingeria elegans is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It belongs to the genus Doellingeria, which is a group of flowering plants native to North America. The species is further divided into several subspecies, each with unique characteristics and distribution ranges. Within the plant kingdom, Doellingeria elegans is classified as a perennial herb, characterized by its simple leaves and daisy-like flowers. This classification places it within the larger hierarchy of the plant kingdom, with Doellingeria elegans being a specific and distinct species within its genus and family.
uses: Doellingeria elegans, also known as panicled aster, is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits.
